raw_response:
I agree with the prior conclusion that option A (B-cell) is correct because B-cell lymphomas constitute approximately 60–70% of orbital NHL cases, primarily driven by their association with extranodal diseases such as MALT lymphomas, which tend to localize around areas like the orbit due to its close anatomical relationship with the paranasal sinuses. Additionally, B-cell malignancies represent the majority of all NHL types globally, further supporting their higher incidence in orbital locations compared to other subtypes.


Based on the above, I would select **<A>**, since there is strong epidemiological data showing that B-cell lymphomas predominate among orbital NHL cases.
